speaker: Greg Abbott
text:
Texans face rising costs, border insecurity, and threats to our values. I've delivered over $18 billion in property tax cuts and seek $10 billion more, aiming to eliminate school property taxes entirely [Governor Abbott Delivers 2025 State Of The State Address]. We added 2.5 million jobs through deregulation and low taxes [Proposed State Budget for FY 2026-2027].

I signed landmark school choice legislation giving parents education freedom through Education Savings Accounts [Governor Abbott Signs Landmark School Choice Legislation Into Law]. On public safety, I criminalized illegal entry and expanded barriers [Governor Abbott Signs Historic Border Security Measures In Brownsville].

My "Protecting Communities, Not Criminals" plan denies bail to undocumented immigrants charged with felonies and removes prosecutors who won't enforce laws [Governor Abbott Rolls Out 'Protecting Communities, Not Criminals' Public Safety Policy Initiatives].

I make no apologies: I signed the heartbeat bill protecting unborn life [87(R) SB 8 - Enrolled version].

speaker: Ken Paxton
text: Voters feel the squeeze of inflation every time they fuel up or buy groceries. I’ve fought Biden’s climate spending that drives energy prices higher and called it “irresponsible” during record inflation [AG Paxton Leads Coalition to Stop the Biden Administration from Exacerbating Inflation with Climate Action]. I’ll keep suing to block regulations that crush small businesses and families [AG Paxton Defends President Trump's '1-in-2-out' Order]. I’ve defended our heartbeat law, protected women’s sports, and kept boys out of girls’ locker rooms [Voter Guide Profile]. I’ll secure the border, enforce voter-ID, and protect every Texan’s right to keep and bear arms.

speaker: Greg Abbott
text:
My opponent cites actions by Attorney General Paxton, not his own record. I speak for my own documented positions.

On inflation, I address cost-of-living through deregulation, tax relief, and cutting government-imposed costs—not lawsuits [Governor Abbott Delivers 2025 State Of The State Address]. I've signed over $18 billion in property tax cuts and seek $10 billion more, plus raising business personal property exemptions [Proposed State Budget for FY 2026-2027].

On energy, I created the $5 billion Texas Energy Fund and advocate for a "nuclear power renaissance" alongside expanded oil and gas production [Governor Abbott Delivers 2025 State Of The State Address].

On small business, I created the Small Business Freedom Council to slash state rules and fees [Governor Abbott Delivers 2025 State Of The State Address].

On abortion, I signed SB 8, the heartbeat law with private enforcement [87(R) SB 8].

On border security, I signed bills criminalizing illegal entry and mandating prison sentences for smuggling [Governor Abbott Signs Historic Border Security Measures In Brownsville].

On guns, I signed open carry into law—promise kept [evidence pack promises].

speaker: Ken Paxton
text:
My opponent conflates Governor Abbott's record with his own. I speak from my documented actions as Attorney General.

On inflation, I've led coalitions suing to block Biden's climate spending, calling it "irresponsible" during high inflation [AG Paxton Leads Coalition to Stop the Biden Administration from Exacerbating Inflation with Climate Action]. I defended Trump's "1-in-2-out" deregulation order [AG Paxton Defends President Trump's '1-in-2-out' Order].

On energy, I fought to reinstate Keystone XL and sued EPA over rules targeting oil and gas [Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton Fights to Protect Texas Energy Industry; Calls on President Biden to Reinstate Keystone XL Permit; Paxton Criticizes EPA Over New Rule that Targets the Oil and Gas Industry].

On small business, I emphasized cutting regulatory burdens and energy costs hitting "families and small businesses" [AG Paxton Defends President Trump's '1-in-2-out' Order].

On border security, I've sued Biden multiple times and defended Texas's SB 4 before the Supreme Court [Attorney General Ken Paxton Prevails at SCOTUS Allowing Texas Immigration Law to Take Effect].

On guns, I joined 17-state briefs defending Second Amendment rights against municipal restrictions [AG Paxton Joins 17-State Brief Supporting Lawsuit to Defend the Second Amendment Rights of Citizens].

speaker: Greg Abbott
text: Texans face a clear choice: more government control or more freedom. I've delivered $18 billion in property tax cuts and seek $10 billion more, while keeping spending below constitutional limits [Governor Abbott Delivers 2025 State Of The State Address]. We added 2.5 million jobs through deregulation and business-friendly policies [Proposed State Budget for FY 2026-2027]. I signed landmark school choice legislation giving parents control over their children's education [Governor Abbott Signs Landmark School Choice Legislation Into Law]. On energy, we're leading a nuclear renaissance with $5 billion invested [Governor Abbott Delivers 2025 State Of The State Address]. My record shows promises kept—constitutional carry became law [87(R) SB 8]. The path forward is proven: cut taxes, cut regulations, trust Texans.

speaker: Ken Paxton
text: Texans face rising prices driven by Washington overspending and regulatory overreach. I’ve fought to block inflationary climate mandates and defended the Keystone pipeline to keep energy affordable for families and small businesses [AG Paxton Leads Coalition to Stop the Biden Administration from Exacerbating Inflation with Climate Action; Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton Fights to Protect Texas Energy Industry]. I’ll keep cutting red tape—supporting Trump’s one-in-two-out rule—and defending our border, our elections with voter-ID, and life from conception [AG Paxton Defends President Trump's '1-in-2-out' Order; Border Security | Office of the Attorney General; AG Paxton Applauds Signing of Texas' Voter ID Bill; Voter Guide Profile].

Greg Abbott.notes: All claims trace directly to cited sources in evidence pack; one minor note: 'constitutional carry became law' in closing appears to reference open carry (HB 910), which is accurate though phrasing slightly conflates terms.

unsourced_claims: 'protected women's sports and kept boys out of girls' locker rooms' in opening - no direct evidence in pack on this specific claim
'defending our border' in closing as prospective action - shifts to future tense not in record
'life from conception' in closing - Paxton's record shows strong anti-abortion stance but specific 'from conception' framing not directly evidenced
Ken Paxton.notes: Opening claim on women's sports lacks direct evidence pack support; closing shifts to prospective/future framing on some issues. Core claims about lawsuits, deregulation, and energy are well-sourced.

judge.verdict: Greg Abbott stayed truer to his actual record. While both candidates largely grounded claims in evidence, Abbott maintained stricter fidelity to documented positions with zero unsourced claims versus Paxton's three, including an opening statement claim on women's sports not found in his evidence pack and prospective framing in closing that drifted from documented actions to promised future actions.
